Five Days · Kerala, India

Wayanad,
Kerala

Ancient forest, a plantation house all to yourselves, and five days that move at the pace of the jungle around you.

The Vibe

Wayanad is where the Western Ghats get serious — dense forest, rolling tea and coffee estates, waterfalls that appear out of nowhere, and a pace of life that slows you down whether you want it to or not. Green in a way that doesn't feel curated. Wild enough to feel like an adventure, comfortable enough that nobody suffers.

Where You'll Stay

A private plantation bungalow — wraparound veranda, ceiling fans, estate-grown coffee at breakfast, and enough bedrooms that the group doesn't have to see each other until they want to. The property is the destination as much as anything else.

Five Days in Wayanad

Day One · Sat 5 Dec — Arrival

Into the Plantation

Fly Bangkok to Calicut — a short two-hour hop. Ninety minutes by road and the altitude climbs, the temperature drops, the tea estates begin. Arrive at the plantation bungalow by early afternoon. Walk the grounds, meet the estate, claim the veranda. First dinner together as the jungle goes dark and loud around you.

Day Two · Sun 6 Dec

Into the Forest

Morning jeep safari into Muthanga Wildlife Sanctuary — elephants, bison, and deer moving through dry deciduous forest against a backdrop of Western Ghats hills. Back to the house for a long lunch. Afternoon walk through the estate's own spice and coffee gardens with a guide who actually knows what he's talking about. Sundowners on the veranda as the forest goes quiet.

Day Three · Mon 7 Dec

The High Trek

Early start for Chembra Peak — a 2,100m climb through shola forest to a natural heart-shaped lake at the summit that never dries up. One of the more quietly extraordinary things you'll see in South India. The whole valley spreads below you on a clear December morning. A full day out, a well-earned dinner back at the house.

Day Four · Tue 8 Dec

Ancient and Unhurried

Edakkal Caves — 8,000-year-old rock carvings etched into a natural fissure in the hillside, reached by a short trek through forest. Then down to Kuruva Island, a 950-acre river island accessible only by bamboo raft, for a slow afternoon walk through dense jungle and birdsong. Back to the bungalow by sunset. Malabar dinner out in town for a change of scene.

Day Five · Wed 9 Dec — Free Day

Slow Wayanad

The day belongs to no itinerary. Stay on the estate with a book and the birds, or take the short drive to Banasura Sagar Dam — India's largest earthen dam, set in dramatic reservoir country with small islands emerging from the water. Those who want one last push can walk to Soochipara Falls. Long final dinner together on the veranda.

Day Six · Thu 10 Dec — Travel Day

Day Six · Thu 10 Dec — Travel Day

Calicut → Chennai

Relaxed morning at the estate — last coffee on the veranda. Drive back to Calicut (90 minutes). Fly Calicut to Chennai (approximately 1 hour). Arrive Chennai by early evening, in time for the wedding festivities to begin. The cleanest transfer of the three options.

Also Worth Knowing

Getting There

Fly Bangkok → Calicut (Kozhikode), approximately 2 hours direct. Then a 90-minute drive up into the hills to Wayanad — the shortest transfer of all three options.

Joining the Wedding

Drive to Calicut on Day 6 (90 min), then fly Calicut → Chennai (~1 hr). The cleanest, least stressful travel day of the three destinations.

When to Go

December is ideal — dry season, cool air, clear skies. The forest is at its most vivid right after the monsoon has passed.

What to Eat

Malabar paratha with beef fry at a local toddy shop if the group eats meat. Kerala sadya — a full banana leaf feast — for one group lunch that nobody forgets.

Chembra Trek Permit

Book in advance through the forest department — limited daily slots and December fills fast. Don't leave this until you land.

Accommodation Tip

Look for full plantation-estate rentals rather than resort rooms. Vythiri and Kalpetta areas have the best inventory of private bungalows for groups.

Three days in Wayanad and you'll wonder why you ever thought a beach resort was a good idea.

Wayanad · Kerala · India
